title: Experience
text:
My experience spans corporate supply chain, nonprofit student services, college access outreach, and data analytics — with a consistent thread of using structure and problem-solving to make programs run better and decisions more informed. Each entry below includes a brief context of the role, what I contributed, and what I took away from it.


  
    
      Student Business Data Analyst
      Anaheim Ducks Hockey Club  ·  Apprenticeship  ·  Hybrid
    
    Jan 2026 – Present  ·  Irvine, CA
  
  This role is part of my UCI MSBA Capstone project, through which I was placed with the Anaheim Ducks and The Rinks — a local hockey rink operator — to analyze whether participation in The Rinks' adult Learn-to-Play program, a low-cost hockey learning initiative, translates into Anaheim Ducks ticket purchases and sustained fan engagement.
  
    Designing an end-to-end analytical framework to evaluate how The Rinks' adult Learn-to-Play program influences Anaheim Ducks ticket purchases and long-term fan engagement across disconnected programming and ticketing systems.
    Writing SQL queries in SSMS to apply client-defined business logic and isolate clean, analysis-ready ticket data, including revenue calculations across primary and secondary market channels and participant filtering across multiple Ticketmaster schema tables.
    Building Power BI dashboards to visualize program retention, pre/post-enrollment ticket purchasing behavior, and fan conversion metrics for Anaheim Ducks business intelligence stakeholders.
  
  Skills Developed: SQL, Power BI, Data Visualization, Business Intelligence, Analytical Framework Design



  
    
      EAOP Program Coordinator
      UCI Center for Educational Partnerships  ·  Full-time  ·  Hybrid
    
    Apr 2025 – Sep 2025  ·  Irvine, CA
  
  UCI's Early Academic Outreach Program (EAOP), housed within the Center for Educational Partnerships, focuses on expanding college access for underrepresented students in the Orange and Los Angeles County regions. In this role, I supported the full range of K-16 programming and outreach initiatives the program delivers throughout the academic year.
  
    Coordinated academic programming and event logistics for key K-16 initiatives such as the Choosing a Major Conference, Southern California UC Tours, Nursing Camp in Summer, and UC Bootcamp; supported outreach, transportation, documentation, and day-of execution.
    Developed a series of curriculum and interactive workshops on interviewing techniques and skills aimed towards high school and community college students, incorporating hands-on activities and practical training.
    Produced and hosted a multi-part webinar series focused on UCI's nursing, business, and engineering pathways, guiding high school students through major requirements, career paths, and pathway structure.
  
  Skills Developed: Program Coordination, Curriculum Development, Video Production, Event Logistics, Public Speaking



  
    
      Program Coordinator
      UCI Basic Needs Center  ·  Full-time  ·  Hybrid
    
    Jan 2024 – Aug 2024  ·  Irvine, CA
  
  Upon graduating from UCI, I was promoted from Basic Needs Advocate to the full-time Program Coordinator role. I took on direct oversight of the Basic Needs Advocate team, all CalFresh initiatives and day-to-day operations, and the center's marketing efforts — and made it a priority to introduce data analytics into how we tracked and optimized CalFresh assistance appointment outcomes.
  
    Managed data entry and analysis for CalFresh services, implementing a new Excel system that tracked eligibility data, reporting timelines, and confidential case details, resulting in a 50% improvement in data management efficiency.
    Directed and managed food security initiatives such as CalFresh Assistance Appointments, Fresh Finances, and CalFresh Enrollment Parties, overseeing logistics, resource organization, scheduling, stakeholder communication, daily operations, and targeted outreach.
    Led scheduling, task delegation, and hiring/training for CalFresh and marketing student teams; optimized workflow, enhanced team efficiency by 50%, and ensured the timely completion of projects in high-pressure environments.
    Directed marketing initiatives across digital and print platforms, leveraging WordPress, Mailchimp, Canva, and Meta Business Suite; led UCI's 2024 Giving Day campaign that raised record-breaking funds and increased social media engagement by 200%.
  
  Skills Developed: Project Management, Data Analysis, Marketing, Team Leadership, Microsoft Excel, Canva, Mailchimp, Meta Business Suite



  
    
      Basic Needs Advocate
      UCI Basic Needs Center  ·  Student Role  ·  Hybrid
    
    Mar 2022 – Dec 2023  ·  Irvine, CA
  
  A student-facing role at the UCI Basic Needs Center, focused on providing direct support to underserved students through CalFresh consultations and in-person outreach. CalFresh is California's name for the federal Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program (SNAP), commonly known as food stamps.
  
    Provided front-desk support and confidential student consultations to help students apply for CalFresh, as well as connecting them to resources related to academics, employment, budgeting, credit and banking, financial aid, housing, loans, and transportation.
    Supported day-to-day event operations including set-up, tear-down, and in-person outreach for various Basic Needs and CalFresh-related public campaigns and events.
  
  Skills Developed: Community Outreach, Interpersonal Communication, Student Services, Case Management



  
    
      Education Department Intern
      Ronald Reagan Presidential Foundation & Institute  ·  Internship  ·  On-site
    
    Jun 2023 – Aug 2023  ·  Simi Valley, CA
  
  Having participated in the Reagan Presidential Library's Summer Leadership Program (SLP) as a high school student, I returned years later to lead it as an intern. The SLP is a week-long program aimed at teaching middle and high school students the tenets of leadership and community service, and I worked alongside a team of five interns and educators to bring the 2023 cohort to life.
  
    Led SLP student sessions with 40+ students in attendance per session, delivering a full week of back-to-back programming that included an intricate curriculum of lessons, guided museum tours, and hands-on activities spanning the entire Reagan Presidential Library and Museum.   
    Developed student public speaking and collaboration skills through interactive lessons, group projects, and a community action plan the students designed and presented to local community members.
    Facilitated numerous Reagan-sponsored events including the GE-Reagan Foundation Scholarship Program and the Great Communicator Debate Series.
  
  Skills Developed: Collaborative Problem Solving, Presentation Skills, Curriculum Design, Event Facilitation, Youth Education



  
    
      Supply Chain Intern
      Chipotle Mexican Grill  ·  Internship  ·  On-site
    
    Jun 2022 – Aug 2022  ·  Newport Beach, CA
  
  A traditional corporate internship structure that included coffee chats, team meetings, and various data tasks — with one standout responsibility: leading an ingredient mapping project that I ultimately presented to Chipotle's executive team at the end of the summer.
  
    Led a comprehensive ingredient mapping initiative, analyzing product specifications, pull cadence, and pricing data from over 40 tier-two suppliers, 150 sub-ingredients, and 6 commissaries to optimize supply chain and procurement processes.
    Identified cost-saving opportunities by consolidating ingredients for cumin, salt, and black pepper, contributing to a more streamlined procurement process and potential cost reductions.
    Managed the daily compilation and analysis of inventory and supply data from nationwide distribution centers, improving visibility and communication between CMG's commissaries and distribution centers.
  
  Skills Developed: Supply Chain Analysis, Microsoft Excel, Data Compilation, Procurement, Inventory Management

title: Welcome to my Website!
text: Hello, my name is Allan Almaraz. I am a first-generation graduate student pursuing my Master of Science in Business Analytics (MSBA) at the University of California, Irvine (UCI), with a passion for using data to inform decisions and drive meaningful change. This is my professional portfolio, where you can explore who I am, my experience, and the projects I have worked on. Welcome, and please take a look around!

title: Welcome to my Website!
text:
Personal Mission Statement
I believe data has the power to move organizations forward. My goal is to contribute to the kinds of companies that shape everyday life, the brands and institutions that people interact with through the products they buy, the food they eat, and the places they work and learn. I find purpose in knowing that my analysis could influence how these organizations operate, and that the impact of that work would be visible in the world around me. Whether supporting a restaurant brand, an apparel company, an automotive manufacturer, or a university, I aspire to be the analyst who turns complex data into decisions that actually change how things are done. For me, that is what makes analytics meaningful.

title: Welcome to my Website!
text:
Goals & Target Opportunities
Through my MSBA program and professional experience, I have developed a growing toolkit centered on data analysis and business strategy, and I am intentionally using it as a pivot into more technical, analytical work. By deepening my expertise in R, SQL, Python, Tableau, and Power BI, my goal is to bridge the analytical and strategic sides of a business: not just reporting what happened, but informing what should happen next. With a graduation date of June 2026, I am actively pursuing entry-level analyst roles in financial, marketing, and business analytics across industries like food service, higher education, and consumer goods.




  
    Target Roles
    Financial Analyst
    Budget Analyst
    Marketing Analyst
    Supply Chain Analyst
    Business Strategy Analyst
  
  
    Target Industries
    Restaurant & Food Service
    Higher Education
    Local Government
    Consumer Packaged Goods
    Apparel
  
  
    Target Regions

    Santa Barbara County
    Sacramento County
    Orange County

title: About Me
text: Hello! I’m Allan, I am a first-generation graduate student pursuing my Master of Science in Business Analytics (MSBA) at UC Irvine. Driven by a belief that data has the power to move organizations forward, my goal is to contribute to companies that shape everyday life, using analytics to inform decisions that actually change how things are done.

title: About Me
text:
Personal Background


  
    
  
  
    
  
  
    
  

I was raised in Santa Paula, CA, a small agricultural city in Ventura County with a tight-knit Latino community, by two Mexican immigrant parents who worked day and night to put food on the table. From a young age, my siblings and I worked alongside them in their side ventures, including recycling, landscaping, installing water systems, welding, and painting. We were either in school or working, and while that was demanding, it was also grounding. My parents were strict, expected a great deal from us, and were deeply supportive all at once. School was always the top priority in our home, and that emphasis paid off. My siblings and I have all graduated from college and successfully entered a professional world that once excluded people like our parents.
As I have grown, I have tried to give back in the same spirit they gave to us. I took over managing their finances, helped map out their long-term goals, and started teaching them practical skills like basic technology and U.S. history as they prepare for their citizenship exam. When I noticed their credit card debt was becoming a burden, I developed a plan to help them pay it off and used it as an opportunity to teach them about credit and personal finance. As I continue to grow professionally, I hope to keep finding new ways to make sure they are never left behind.

title: Projects
text:
This is where I showcase my work as an analyst. Each project below tackles a real business question, walks through the full analytical process, and delivers findings that go beyond the numbers. The work spans three tracks: Predictive Analytics, where I build and evaluate models to forecast outcomes; Data Visualization, where the goal is a dashboard or visual that makes patterns immediately clear to a stakeholder; and Live Dashboards, ongoing data products that update automatically and are designed to be used repeatedly, not just once. Every project card links to its own dedicated page covering methodology, tools, findings, and deliverables.
Predictive Analytics

  
    
      ✈️
      Complete
    
    Sun Country Airlines — Customer Segmentation
    Business question: Who are Sun Country's customers, and how can the airline grow loyalty enrollment and shift bookings to its direct channel?
    Identified 5 customer segments from booking and loyalty data to support Ufly enrollment growth and direct channel strategy.
    
      Segmentation
      Customer analytics
      Business strategy
    
    
      
        Python
        pandas
        scikit-learn
      
      View →
    
  
  
    
      👥
      Complete
    
    Predicting Employee Churn
    Business question: Which employees are most at risk of leaving, and what factors drive that risk?
    Built logistic regression and decision tree models to predict turnover, revealing tenure, compensation, and education as key risk drivers.
